page: Microsoft Azure D64s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache. The system is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64s_v6 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, achieving top-tier status in Geekbench multi-core performance and image processing. Its memory subsystem shows strong throughput, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 259.69 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 110.72 GB/sec. The processor's advanced instruction support, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, drives strong performance in cryptographic operations, ray tracing, and LLM inference. Specifically, prompt processing for a medium 7B model reaches 270.18 tokens/sec. Database operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier, with web serving reaching 97.36 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64s_v6 offers a balanced resource profile, providing high-density compute and memory resources without the added cost of bundled local NVMe storage or specialized GPU hardware. This makes it an efficient option for workloads that rely on network-attached premium storage. The instance is suited for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, including database operations, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv6 family, powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM. The instance features no local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. In benchmarks, the D64s_v6 excels in multi-core processing, database operations, and static web serving, placing in the top tier for Geekbench multi-core and web throughput. Supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, it provides strong memory bandwidth and efficient cryptographic performance. This configuration is optimized for demanding enterprise workloads such as database management, multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier database and multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for database and web workloads.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose virtual machine delivering exceptional multi-core performance, massive memory bandwidth, and advanced instruction acceleration for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor.
256.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
Top-tier performance in database operations and static web serving benchmarks.
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for accelerated workloads.
Premium Storage capable configuration with no local storage or onboard GPUs.
Optimized for enterprise databases, software compilation, and CPU-based inference.
